evals.prompt: I'm applying to a backend engineer role at Kakao in Seoul. I've got my CV in resumes/ but it's in English one-page ATS format. Can you generate a proper Korean-style resume that shows my full project experience? The Kakao role emphasizes microservices and cloud infrastructure.
evals.expected_output: A multi-page Korean resume (2-4 pages) in PDF format at output/kakao/backend-engineer/ with proper section layout (header, About Me, Skill Set, Work Experience, Projects). Korean glyphs should render correctly, with project sections grouped by company.

assertions.name: mentions_skill_workflow
assertions.description: Response references the korean-resume-builder workflow (gather source facts → choose layout variant → translate to Korean → fill HTML asset → generate PDF)

assertions.name: tailoring_for_company
assertions.description: Response specifically mentions tailoring for Kakao's requirements (microservices, cloud infrastructure) or emphasizes role-specific content selection

assertions.name: korean_layout_pattern
assertions.description: Response identifies the 'experienced developer pattern' or multi-page layout structure, NOT one-page ATS format

assertions.name: output_path_specificity
assertions.description: Response suggests or discusses output path structure (e.g., output/{company-slug}/{role-slug}/ or output/kakao/)

assertions.name: korean_formatting_details
assertions.description: Response mentions Korean date format (YYYY. MM ~), section labels in Korean, or other Korean-specific formatting concerns

evals.prompt: I want to create a general Korean resume that I can use for multiple applications to Korean companies. I'm mid-career with 5 years of full-stack experience. Use my CV from resumes/ and make it look like the Jumpit reference examples. 2-3 pages, clean layout.
evals.expected_output: A polished Korean resume PDF at output/korean-resume/ using the Jumpit layout style (experienced developer pattern). Clean typography, proper Korean date format (YYYY. MM ~), skill table, and project sections with links.

assertions.name: references_jumpit_style
assertions.description: Response references the Jumpit layout examples or the experienced developer pattern (page 1: header, About Me, Skill Set, Work Experience; later pages: Projects)

assertions.name: cv_source_identification
assertions.description: Response mentions which CV file from resumes/ would be used or describes the process of selecting the best-matching CV

assertions.name: multi_page_structure
assertions.description: Response indicates 2-4 page structure, NOT one-page compression, and describes how projects would be organized across pages

assertions.name: korean_content_guidance
assertions.description: Response discusses translation approach (concise bullets, factual statements) or mentions keeping Korean content honest/verifiable

assertions.name: output_path_clarity
assertions.description: Response suggests output path (e.g., output/korean-resume/ or output/general-korean/) for a general-use resume

assertions.name: verification_steps
assertions.description: Response mentions visual review steps (page count, Korean glyph rendering, table alignment, page breaks) for quality assurance

evals.prompt: Can you help me decide if I should use the Korean resume builder for a Naver job posting? The role is 'Senior Full Stack Developer' and they posted it in Korean on their careers page.
evals.expected_output: Claude should identify this as a Korean-market role and ask clarifying questions: (1) whether the user wants a one-page ATS CV or multi-page Korean format, (2) confirm they have CV data in resumes/, and (3) optionally fetch the JD to tailor the resume. Should NOT auto-generate without user confirmation.

assertions.name: asks_clarifying_questions
assertions.description: Response asks at least 2 clarifying questions (e.g., one-page vs. multi-page preference, confirm CV exists in resumes/, whether to tailor for Naver specifically)

assertions.name: does_not_autogenerate
assertions.description: Response does NOT auto-generate a resume PDF or HTML without explicit user confirmation; stops before taking action

assertions.name: explains_decision_criteria
assertions.description: Response explains WHEN to use korean-resume-builder (Korean-market role, multi-page format) vs. modes/pdf.md (ATS one-page)

assertions.name: identifies_korean_context
assertions.description: Response recognizes that a Korean-language job posting from a Korean company (Naver) is appropriate for the korean-resume-builder skill

assertions.name: recommends_next_steps
assertions.description: Response provides clear next steps (fetch the JD, confirm preferences, gather CV data) rather than vague advice
